message: I've been digging into home fire prep lately, and honestly, there's something reassuring about having a solid plan in place—it's one of those things that feels overwhelming until you actually start. Found this solid resource from Ready.gov that breaks down the practical stuff in a way that doesn't feel scary, just... doable. What's one fire-safety thing you've already got handled at your place, or what's been on your to-do list?
